file.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

229-
230-    Parameters
231-    ----------

233-        The regular expression as a string.
234-
235-    """
236-    pattern: str
237-
238-    def _display_node(self) -> str:
239-        return f"Regex('{self.pattern}')"
240-
241-    def __repr__(self):
242-        return f"Regex(pattern='{self.pattern}')"
243-
244-
245-@dataclass
246-class CFG(Term):
247-    """Class representing a context-free grammar.
248-

252-        The definition of the context-free grammar as a string.
253-
254-    """
255-    definition: str
256-

262-
263-    def __eq__(self, other):
264-        if not isinstance(other, CFG):
265-            return False
266-        return self.definition == other.definition

77:class Term:
78-    """Represents types defined with a regular expression.
79-
80-    `Regex` instances can be used as a type in a Pydantic model definittion.
81-    They will be translated to JSON Schema as a "string" field with the
82-    "pattern" keyword set to the regular expression this class represents. The
83-    class also handles validation.
84-
85-    Examples
86-    --------
87-
88-    >>> from outlines.types import Regex
89-    >>> from pydantic import BaseModel
90-    >>>
91-    >>> age_type = Regex("[0-9]+")
92-    >>>
--
124:        def validate(input_value):
125-            return self.validate(input_value)
126-
127-        return validate
128-
129-    def __get_pydantic_core_schema__(
130-        self, source_type: Any, handler: GetCoreSchemaHandler
131-    ) -> cs.CoreSchema:
132-        return cs.no_info_plain_validator_function(lambda value: self.validate(value))
133-
134-    def __get_pydantic_json_schema__(
135-        self, core_schema: cs.CoreSchema, handler: GetJsonSchemaHandler
136-    ) -> JsonSchemaValue:
137-        return {"type": "string", "pattern": to_regex(self)}
138-
139:    def validate(self, value: str) -> str:
140-        pattern = to_regex(self)
141-        compiled = re.compile(pattern)
142-        if not compiled.fullmatch(str(value)):
143-            raise ValueError(
144-                f"Input should be in the language of the regular expression {pattern}"
145-            )
146-        return value
147-
148:    def matches(self, value: str) -> bool:
149-        """Check that a given value is in the language defined by the Term.
150-
151-        We make the assumption that the language defined by the term can
152-        be defined with a regular expression.
153-
154-        """
155-        pattern = to_regex(self)
156-        compiled = re.compile(pattern)
157-        if compiled.fullmatch(str(value)):
158-            return True
159-        return False
160-
161-    def display_ascii_tree(self, indent="", is_last=True) -> str:
162-        """Display the regex tree in ASCII format."""
163-        branch = "└── " if is_last else "├── "

content.text: Good, `validate`/`matches` use `fullmatch`, so no anchors needed in the pattern itself. Now I'll add the `hash_sha256` type.

tool_use_result.originalFile:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

lines: +# Matches a SHA-256 hash: 64 hexadecimal characters (case-insensitive)

content.text: Now let me verify this behaves correctly and check for `__all__` or export lists elsewhere that might need updating.

content.text: No `__all__` list to update — types are used via `types.isbn` style access. Let me quickly verify the new type works as expected.

input.command:
python -c "
from outlines.types import hash_sha256

valid = 'e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b85'
print(len(valid), hash_sha256.matches(valid))
print(hash_sha256.matches(valid.upper()))
print(hash_sha256.matches(valid[:-1]))  # 63 chars -> False
print(hash_sha256.matches(valid + 'a'))  # 65 chars -> False
print(hash_sha256.matches(''))  # empty -> False
print(hash_sha256.matches(valid.replace('e','g')))  # invalid char -> False
print(hash_sha256.matches(' ' + valid))  # leading whitespace -> False
print(hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__(None, None))
"

input.command:
python -c "
from outlines.types import hash_sha256
import hashlib

valid = hashlib.sha256(b'').hexdigest()
print(len(valid), valid)
print('lowercase full match:', hash_sha256.matches(valid))
print('uppercase full match:', hash_sha256.matches(valid.upper()))
print('mixed case:', hash_sha256.matches(valid[:32].upper() + valid[32:]))
print('63 chars (invalid):', hash_sha256.matches(valid[:-1]))
print('65 chars (invalid):', hash_sha256.matches(valid + 'a'))
print('empty (invalid):', hash_sha256.matches(''))
print('invalid char g (invalid):', hash_sha256.matches(valid.replace('e','g')))
print('leading whitespace (invalid):', hash_sha256.matches(' ' + valid))
print('trailing whitespace (invalid):', hash_sha256.matches(valid + ' '))
"

content.content:
diff --git a/outlines/types/__init__.py b/outlines/types/__init__.py
index 85af9adc..924caa71 100644
--- a/outlines/types/__init__.py
+++ b/outlines/types/__init__.py
@@ -68,3 +68,6 @@ email = Regex(
 isbn = Regex(
     r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
 )
+
+# Matches a SHA-256 hash: 64 hexadecimal characters (case-insensitive)
+hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")

 )
+
+# Matches a SHA-256 hash: 64 hexadecimal characters (case-insensitive)
+hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "STATUS" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --summary "Added hash_sha256 = Regex(r'[a-fA-F0-9]{64}') appended right after isbn definition; both our additions are append-only so should merge fine, just flagging exact insertion point"

content.text: The `hash_sha256` type has been added to `outlines/types/__init__.py`, appended after the existing `isbn` definition. I verified it correctly validates 64-char hex strings (case-insensitive), rejects 63/65-char strings, empty strings, non-hex characters, and leading/trailing whitespace via `fullmatch` semantics in `Term.validate`/`matches`. I also confirmed the pydantic JSON schema output works as expected. The diff is written to `patch.txt`, and I notified agent1 (who is adding an `ssn` type to the same file) about my insertion point to avoid merge conflicts.
